programme.desc: A bear stealing clothes; a stingray with an attitude; an elephant that sprays a woman; a bat that hides in an umbrella.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Harris is forced to confront Darlene about her lifestyle; Jackie involves herself in other people's problems to avoid confronting her own; Becky is overwhelmed by the costs of raising a baby, so her family steps in to help.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Desperate for more income, Becky seeks a bartending position at Casita Bonita, causing major concern for Dan due to her struggle with alcoholism; Darlene finally nears a decision between David and Ben; Harris' new friend, Odessa, means new trouble.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: The actors in Randy's (Jonathan Taylor Thomas) school play become frustrated when Wilson (Earl Hindman) takes over as director.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim (Tim Allen) becomes concerned when his mother (guest star Bonnie Bartlett) begins dating his former shop teacher (guest star Dick O'Neill).

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: The family becomes annoyed with the new home alarm system when Jill decides she wants it to have more power.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Al's dreams of success and proposing to Ilene may be snuffed out when the Taylors discover a defect in his board game.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.sub-title: Sleeping Next to an Old Boat
programme.desc: Abishola worries that Bob's privileged lifestyle is starting to rub off on Dele; a new robot vacuum brings Tunde joy, but begins to make Olu feel insecure.

programme.title: Bob Hearts Abishola
programme.sub-title: The Genius Who Fell Out of My Womb
programme.desc: Bob is thrilled to share a day at his alma mater with Abishola and Dele, but his university doesn't meet Abishola's Ivy League standards.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: To teach Jim the value of communication, Cheryl does not speak to him for three days.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim tries to blackmail Dana when he learns she is having erotic dreams about him.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Vanessa turns to Mike and Carol for advice on her State Assembly campaign; Kyle is overcome with anxiety when Ed gifts him an expensive watch.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike's offer to help Jen with her school's bake sale goes up in smoke; Kyle finds unlikely advice from Joe on where he should enroll in college.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike plays cupid for Joe when he sets him up on a date with Cece; Kyle is jealous when Ryan invites Jen to be a guest on their podcast.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: When Mandy worries about Kyle passing his first college exam, she secretly enlists Vanessa, Jen and Ryan to help tutor him; Mike learns of Ed and Chuck's superstition involving their decision-making.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim tries to be a cool dad to impress Ruby and her friends but realizes that she is just manipulating him.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: When Jim is prescribed pain pills for a back injury, the family takes advantage of his unusually mellow mood.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: Jim is frustrated that Kyle seems to like science-fiction better than sports, so he tries to change his son's opinion by going to a convention with him.

programme.title: According to Jim
programme.desc: When Jim gets financially rewarded for doing charity work, Cheryl tells him that he should return the money.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.sub-title: Brother, Can You Spare a Hot Rod?
programme.desc: Tim can't think of a reason for selling his hot rod, except money; Randy tries to distance himself from Jill.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Tim throws a Super Bowl party while Jill is bedridden with the flu.

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: Al (Richard Karn) is included among Detroit's sexiest bachelors, and the designation causes problems with Ilene (Sherry Hursey).

programme.title: Home Improvement
programme.desc: When Randy's (Jonathan Taylor Thomas) date falls on the dance floor at his first coed birthday party, Tim (Tim Allen) is to blame.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mike is surprised to learn that he shares similar interests with a new neighbor; Mandy plots revenge after catching her boyfriend in a lie.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: The arrival of the father of Kristen's baby dampens Mike's Christmas spirit; when Ed hires Mandy as an elf, she tries to unionize her co-workers.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: Mandy offers to start an Internet campaign to keep Ed's favorite barber shop open; Vanessa falls asleep during a presentation.

programme.title: Last Man Standing
programme.desc: A visit from a rising star on the professional fishing circuit leaves Vanessa wondering if Mike feels tied down; Eve and Kristin assume Mandy's decision to become a vegan is a passing phase.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric's comely cousin visits, triggering competition for attention among the boys; Red gets Kitty a pet.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric is forced to pal around with Hyde and his female "friend"; Red gives Pastor Dave a new direction in life.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric agrees to direct the church's Christmas pageant, and the casting of his friends leads to disagreements over the interpretation.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Jackie takes a job as a cheese maiden at the Cheese Palace; Eric catches Red combing through his dresser drawers.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: Eric braves a tornado watch to pick up Donna for the prom; the weather warning brings the Formans and Pinciottis together.

programme.title: That '70s Show
programme.desc: All reminisce about meeting one another for the first time, as they prepare to get their class pictures taken.

programme.title: Washington Week With The Atlantic
programme.desc: A panel of journalists provides reporting and analysis of the major stories emanating from the United States capital.

programme.title: Firing Line With Margaret Hoover
programme.desc: Pulitzer Prize-winning historian Jon Meacham examines America's triumphs and tragedies, the tension between patriotism and nationalism and challenges to democracy.

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores plants in the tropical rain forest.

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: Exploring water plants, some of which can count and hunt; some water plants escape from animals by rolling away or creating bubbles.

programme.title: Ancient Skies
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between humans and the skies, from how ancestors used the sky to navigate and tell time to the birth of modern science.

programme.title: Ancient Skies
programme.desc: Tracking major changes in scientific understanding about Earth, from flat Earth legends to Galileo's telescope.

programme.title: Ancient Skies
programme.desc: Moments in history that reshape the solar system and provide an understanding of the universe.

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: Basketball; the Neighbors prepare to celebrate the arrival of the comet.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: What Scared Sue Ellen?; Clarissa Is Cracked
programme.desc: Sue Ellen is frightened by a howling noise coming from the woods; D.W. has fun playing with her grandmother's porcelain doll.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: The Kratt brothers search for a mysterious nocturnal creature, using night-vision goggles.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: Benny's toy penguin is swept away by melting snow, transforming a day of sledding into an urgent rescue mission.

programme.title: Wowsabout
